warnings: Audited financial statements (Exhibit G) are referenced but not included in the provided pages; financial condition fields populated from Item 21 reference only — actual balance sheet figures are null.

Item 19 discloses gross contract revenue and gross revenue tables but no EBITDA/net-income table; profitability data is limited to 'Gross Revenue Less Certain Operating Expenses' which is not a full net-income figure.

extracted.brandBackground: Jan-Pro has offered regional developer franchises for commercial janitorial and building maintenance services since 1995, operating as a sub-franchisor model where regional developers sell and support unit franchises under the JAN-PRO Cleaning & Disinfecting brand; the franchisor is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Empower Brands Franchising, LLC, backed by MidOcean Partners private equity.
extracted.conceptRationale: Regional developer franchise selling and supporting commercial janitorial/building-maintenance unit franchises — a B2B trade services model with no retail storefront.
extracted.staffingRationale: Item 15 requires a full-time Manager with day-to-day supervision plus office administration and sales staff, making this a conventionally staffed business.

leadership.name: Gary Bauer
leadership.role: Brand President, Jan-Pro Franchising International, Inc.
leadership.background: Brand President since April 2020, based in Alpharetta, GA.
leadership.whyItMatters: Top decision-maker for the Jan-Pro regional developer franchise system.

leadership.name: Volker Wellmann
leadership.background: SVP Operations since September 2025; previously VP Operations at Marsden Services and led OneNorth IFS national janitorial division.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees field operations and franchisee support across the network.

leadership.name: Scott Zide
leadership.background: CEO of Empower Brands since March 2022; previously President/COO of Outdoor Living Brands entities for over a decade.
leadership.whyItMatters: Ultimate strategic authority over Jan-Pro's parent company.

leadership.name: Michael Borreca
leadership.role: Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, Empower Brands
leadership.background: CFO of Empower Brands since March 2017; previously VP Corporate Finance at FOCUS Brands and KPMG.
leadership.whyItMatters: Controls financial reporting and capital allocation for the parent entity.

leadership.name: Sanjay B. Malhotra
leadership.role: Vice President, General Counsel, Empower Brands
leadership.background: General Counsel since August 2022; previously CLO at Paris Baguette and Global General Counsel at Le Pain Quotidien.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees legal compliance, franchise agreements, and litigation risk.

leadership.name: Neeraj Gupta
leadership.role: Vice President of Training, Technical Development and Sourcing
leadership.background: VP Technical Development, Training and Sourcing since September 2020; 35+ years at ServiceMaster in product/process development.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads franchisee certification programs and product/supplier standards.

item19.notes: Item 19 discloses gross contract revenue (total unit-franchisee billings) and gross revenue (RFD's own recurring contractual revenue from unit franchisees) for 115 RFDs (FY ending Sept 30 2025) and 48 reporting RFDs (CY ending Dec 31 2024). A 'Gross Revenue Less Certain Operating Expenses' table is provided for Tier 3/4 markets but is not a full EBITDA/net-income figure; no separate EBITDA table is disclosed. Tables F–L cover Tier 3 and Tier 4 sub-groups. None of the reported RFDs participated in the Service Agreement Pilot.

cohorts.label: All RFDs – Total Gross Contract Revenue (FY Sept 30 2025)
cohorts.basis: Average annual gross contract revenue (total unit-franchisee billings) for all 115 RFDs operating the full fiscal year ending September 30, 2025; median $5,193,488.

cohorts.label: Top Third RFDs – Gross Contract Revenue (FY Sept 30 2025)
cohorts.basis: Average annual gross contract revenue for the top-third of 115 RFDs by revenue, FY ending September 30, 2025; median $10,292,700.

cohorts.label: Middle Third RFDs – Gross Contract Revenue (FY Sept 30 2025)
cohorts.basis: Average annual gross contract revenue for the middle-third of 115 RFDs, FY ending September 30, 2025; median $5,193,488.

cohorts.label: Bottom Third RFDs – Gross Contract Revenue (FY Sept 30 2025)
cohorts.basis: Average annual gross contract revenue for the bottom-third of 115 RFDs, FY ending September 30, 2025; median $2,031,729.

cohorts.label: All Reporting RFDs – Gross Revenue (CY Dec 31 2024)
cohorts.basis: Average annual gross revenue (RFD's own recurring contractual revenue from unit franchisees) for 48 of 94 U.S. RFDs reporting for CY ending December 31, 2024; median $1,734,513.

cohorts.label: Top Third Reporting RFDs – Gross Revenue (CY Dec 31 2024)
cohorts.basis: Average annual gross revenue for top-third of 48 reporting RFDs, CY 2024; median $3,637,047.

cohorts.label: Middle Third Reporting RFDs – Gross Revenue (CY Dec 31 2024)
cohorts.basis: Average annual gross revenue for middle-third of 48 reporting RFDs, CY 2024; median $1,734,513.

cohorts.label: Bottom Third Reporting RFDs – Gross Revenue (CY Dec 31 2024)
cohorts.basis: Average annual gross revenue for bottom-third of 48 reporting RFDs, CY 2024; median $824,302.

cohorts.label: Tier 3 & 4 RFDs – Gross Contract Revenue (FY Sept 30 2025)
cohorts.basis: Average annual gross contract revenue for 60 RFDs in Tier 3 or 4 markets, FY ending September 30, 2025; median $3,387,173.

cohorts.label: Tier 3 & 4 Reporting RFDs – Gross Revenue Less Certain Operating Expenses (CY Dec 31 2024)
cohorts.basis: Average 'Total Gross Revenue Less Certain Operating Expenses' (gross revenue minus royalties/fees, occupancy, and SG&A) for 19 Tier 3/4 reporting RFDs, CY 2024; excludes owner compensation and staff wages — not a full net-income figure.

cohorts.label: Tier 3 Reporting RFDs – Gross Revenue Less Certain Operating Expenses (CY Dec 31 2024)
cohorts.basis: Average gross revenue less certain operating expenses for 9 Tier 3 reporting RFDs, CY 2024; excludes owner/staff compensation.

cohorts.label: Tier 4 Reporting RFDs – Gross Revenue Less Certain Operating Expenses (CY Dec 31 2024)
cohorts.basis: Average gross revenue less certain operating expenses for 10 Tier 4 reporting RFDs, CY 2024; excludes owner/staff compensation.

lineItems.notes: Paid at signing; non-refundable; may be partially financed by franchisor.
3 months' rent (first, last, security) for 1,500–2,000 sq ft office; Item 7 note 2.
Includes commercial carpet extractor, floor polisher, wet/dry vacuum, and office furnishings; Item 7 note 3.

lineItems.category: Business Licenses & Permits, Deposits & Other Prepaid Expenses
lineItems.notes: Government filing fees, security deposits, and prepaid expenses; Item 7 note 4.
Attendee expenses for initial certification in Alpharetta, GA; Item 7 note 6.
FDD preparation, state registration, and attorney fees; Item 7 note 6.
Hardware and required software (MasterView, QuickBooks, Office 365, CRM); Item 7.
Initial local advertising and marketing materials.
Working capital for first 3 months of operations; Item 7 note 7.

flatMonthlyFees.name: Sales Royalty Fee
flatMonthlyFees.source: Item 6, p. A-15 — 10% of initial unit franchise fees, upgrade fees, transfer fees, and principal/interest collected each month

flatMonthlyFees.name: Advertising Contribution (minimum)
flatMonthlyFees.source: Item 6, p. A-15 — lesser of 0.5% of Gross Monthly Revenue or $3,500/month; minimum $450/month

flatMonthlyFees.name: Web Hosting Fee
flatMonthlyFees.source: Item 6, p. A-16 — $80/month for web hosting

flatMonthlyFees.name: Email Service Fee
flatMonthlyFees.source: Item 6, p. A-16 — $50/month for email services

flatMonthlyFees.name: CRM Software Fee
flatMonthlyFees.source: Item 11, p. A-31 — $37/month per user for designated CRM software

flatMonthlyFees.name: Renewal Fee (one-time)
flatMonthlyFees.source: Item 6, p. A-18 — $20,000 due before renewal

hiddenCosts.name: Annual FDD Preparation & State Registration
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisee must annually prepare and register a Unit FDD at their own expense, including attorney fees and state filing fees.
hiddenCosts.source: Item 1, p. A-7; Item 7, p. A-22

hiddenCosts.name: CRM Software (per user/month)
hiddenCosts.description: Required CRM software from designated supplier at $37/month per user.

hiddenCosts.name: National Accounts Support Fee
hiddenCosts.description: 25% of National Account revenue in first month per facility, then 2% of ongoing National Account revenue (plus 2% for Special Services) retained by JPE.
hiddenCosts.source: Item 6, p. A-16; Item 12, p. A-34

hiddenCosts.name: Business Protection Program (insurance)
hiddenCosts.description: Voluntary but near-universal group workers' compensation/liability/bonding program; cost of insurance plus administrative fee.

hiddenCosts.name: Interim Management Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $5,000–$8,000/month if franchisor operates the business due to death, disability, or breach.

hiddenCosts.name: Audit Cost Reimbursement
hiddenCosts.description: Franchisee reimburses audit costs plus understated amounts plus 18% interest if underpayment ≥5%.

hiddenCosts.name: Late Report Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $50 per day for each late monthly report.

hiddenCosts.name: Supplier Testing/Inspection Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $1,000–$2,000 to cover franchisor's costs for testing or inspecting proposed new suppliers.

hiddenCosts.name: Consultation Fee
hiddenCosts.description: $300/day plus travel expenses (up to $350/day) for additional consulting services requested by franchisee.

operationalRisks.title: Employee Misclassification Litigation (DC – Pending)
operationalRisks.description: DC Attorney General suit alleging JPI is a joint employer of unit franchisees; trial scheduled March 30, 2026 — outcome could impose wage/benefit liability on regional developers.

operationalRisks.title: $30M California Class Action Settlement
operationalRisks.description: Jan-Pro paid $30 million in 2024 to settle a 15-year California class action classifying unit franchisees as employees; sets precedent for future misclassification exposure.

operationalRisks.title: Unit Franchisee Misclassification Risk (Special Risk #3)
operationalRisks.description: The legal classification of unit franchisees as independent businesses vs. employees is uncertain; adverse rulings could impose wage, benefit, and tax obligations on regional developers.

operationalRisks.title: Performance Standard – Territory Reduction or Termination
operationalRisks.description: Failure to maintain top-90% Supported System Sales or 10% YOY growth for any 3-month period can result in territory reduction, loss of National Account eligibility, or termination.
operationalRisks.source: Item 12, p. A-34; Special Risks, p. 4

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution (Georgia)
operationalRisks.description: All mediation and arbitration must occur in Georgia, potentially increasing franchisee dispute costs and reducing leverage.
operationalRisks.source: Special Risks, p. 4; Item 17, p. A-41

operationalRisks.title: Spousal Liability Guarantee
operationalRisks.description: Spouse of franchisee owner must personally guarantee all financial obligations, placing marital assets at risk.

operationalRisks.title: No Right to Terminate Agreement
operationalRisks.description: Franchisee has no contractual right to terminate the franchise agreement; only franchisor may terminate for cause.

operationalRisks.title: Renewal on New Terms
operationalRisks.description: Renewal requires signing the then-current franchise agreement, which may contain materially different (higher) royalty and fee terms.

operationalRisks.title: Post-Termination Non-Compete (2 Years in Territory)
operationalRisks.description: Two-year non-compete within the franchisee's territory after termination, expiration, or transfer.

operationalRisks.title: Franchisor Right of First Refusal
operationalRisks.description: Franchisor may match any third-party offer to purchase the franchised business, limiting exit options.

operationalRisks.title: Parent Reacquisition of Territories Post-FY2025
operationalRisks.description: After fiscal year end, Empower Brands reacquired multiple regional developer territories (AL, AZ, GA, IL, KS, LA, MO, NV, OK), reducing the independent franchisee network.

rentDetail.source: Item 7, p. A-22 — office of approximately 1,500–2,000 sq ft required; Real Estate line item $1,500–$5,000 represents 3 months' rent (first, last, security deposit); implied monthly rent derived from range low divided by 3.

riskReasons: 4 high-severity operational tripwires disclosed — see the tripwires section.
notes: Average rent not found; fixed costs may be understated.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $275,750 mid-point build-out, there is a $25,750 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $473,571/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptRationale: Regional developer franchise selling and supporting commercial janitorial/building-maintenance unit franchises — a B2B trade services model with no retail storefront.

benchmark.label: Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Crew utilization, callback/warranty cost, and customer-acquisition cost. Low fixed overhead, but CAC and scheduling efficiency decide profitability.
benchmark.rampNote: 3–9 months to a steady job pipeline.
considerations: Budget materials/subcontractor COGS ~25–40% and field labor ~20–35%.
CAC (lead cost per booked job) is the dominant hidden line — ask Item 20 operators what they actually spend per job.
Confirm territory size, lead-generation expectations, and any required call-center/marketing fees.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 10–25% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Occupancy / rent (not disclosed)
buildup.note: Item 7 disclosed no rent figure — benchmark occupancy applied so the line above isn't overstated

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 29.3–51.2 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Home & trade services (mobile / low-overhead)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Item 7 disclosed no rent — occupancy estimated at the 2–6% category band.
Estimated at the 25–40% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 20–35% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor flagged its own financial condition as a risk. The figures we extracted look comparatively stable, so confirm directly against the most recent statements.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. There is no parent guarantee of performance in this FDD, so Empower Brands Franchising, LLC is not contractually obligated to keep funding the franchisor or backing its commitments to you.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
aggravators: no parent guarantee backing the franchisor's obligations to you
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Flagged on the FDD's "Special Risks" page and grounded in the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
